rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree blocks to contain AGREE syntax, with type scopes for constants, equations, and assumptions/guarantees; implementation scopes for assignments.
generic_rules: Ensure all AGREE variables are grounded in visible AADL features or explicit declarations.
Use only valid AADL feature names and types in AGREE expressions; avoid AADL property names as variables.
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
Validate that every AGREE expression references only visible identifiers or declared helpers.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type contracts use port features directly; implementation contracts use subcomponent paths and outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes; move valid assignments to implementation scope.
Do not reference AADL properties like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports.
